chrome.deliveryLede: The sequence of work. Timing adapts to the project's scope — always tailored.
chrome.ctaSub: Free first call. A clear answer in 20 minutes.

agents.name: AI Agents
agents.sub: We build custom agents that talk to your customers, automate back-office work or execute tasks inside your stack. They reason with real context — not chatbots with FAQs.
agents.whatBody: An agent is a system that receives an instruction, plans the steps, consults tools (CRM, ERP, knowledge bases) and delivers an outcome or a decision. We design the full cycle — prompting, architecture, memory, evaluation — and integrate it into your daily operations.
agents.forWhoTitle: If you recognise any of these scenarios:
forWho: You get hundreds of repetitive support queries and want a clean automated first tier with proper escalation.
You have internal processes (quoting, onboarding, triage) that depend on living rules and scattered documentation.
Your sales team loses hours filling in the CRM, sending follow-ups or qualifying leads.
You want to leverage internal knowledge bases (wikis, drives, tickets) with reliable, traceable answers.
agents.capsLede: An agent is born from a specific problem, not a technology. These are the patterns we excel at.

capabilities.name: Support agents
capabilities.desc: First-line support with access to your knowledge base, tickets and CRM. Resolves, classifies or escalates with context.

capabilities.name: Sales agents
capabilities.desc: They qualify leads, enrich data, send follow-ups and prep meetings. Integrated with your CRM.

capabilities.name: Operational agents
capabilities.desc: Automate internal processes: onboarding, KYC, triage, quoting. With human checkpoints.

capabilities.name: Knowledge assistants
capabilities.desc: RAG on your documents: wikis, manuals, contracts. Answers with verifiable citations.

capabilities.name: Multi-agent
capabilities.desc: Orchestration of multiple specialised agents collaborating on complex multi-step tasks.

capabilities.name: Voice & phone
capabilities.desc: Voice agents for smart IVRs, call screening and automated bookings.

delivery.name: Technical discovery
delivery.desc: Mapping the process, available data, systems to integrate and success criteria.

delivery.name: Functional prototype
delivery.desc: First operational agent for the priority use case, evaluated against real examples.

delivery.name: Integration & production
delivery.desc: Hook-up with CRM/ERP, auth, logs, rate limits and channels (web, Slack, WhatsApp).

delivery.name: Evolution
delivery.desc: Observability, evals, cost optimisation and expansion to new flows.

faq.q: What's the difference between an agent and a chatbot?
faq.a: A chatbot follows scripts or FAQs. An agent reasons with context, accesses systems (CRM, ERP), makes decisions and executes actions. When the environment changes, it adapts without rewriting rules.

faq.q: Which model do you use?
faq.a: Whatever fits best for each case. We pick between frontier LLMs for complex reasoning, multi-modal models for voice, and smaller models for low-cost tasks. We are vendor- and version-agnostic.

faq.q: What if the agent makes mistakes or hallucinates?
faq.a: We build continuous evaluation systems and human checkpoints for critical cases. Every response is logged with reasoning and sources so you can audit and improve.

faq.q: Where is the data hosted?
faq.a: It depends on sensitivity. We offer EU deployments, dedicated infrastructure or hybrid setups. No data leaves the perimeter unless necessary, always with your explicit consent.

faq.q: Can I migrate the agent if I ever leave?
faq.a: Yes. Code, prompts, data and configuration are yours from day one. We support the migration if needed.

agents.ctaTitle: Want an agent that handles all this work?

n8n.name: n8n Automations
n8n.sub: We design and maintain robust automations that remove repetitive work — from syncing CRM with billing to orchestrating complex AI pipelines. With real observability and maintenance.
n8n.whatBody: Most no-code flows die for lack of maintenance, logging or error handling. We build ours with the standard of a software team: version control, environments, monitoring and documentation. They survive Monday morning.
forWho: Your team copies data between tools every week (CRM, ERP, billing tools, database).
You have a process that starts with an email, a form or a webhook and ends across multiple systems.
You want to add AI steps (classification, extraction, summarisation) to existing flows without rebuilding the architecture.
You have Zapier or Make flows that break often and you want to migrate to reliable infrastructure.
n8n.capsLede: Every case is unique, but there are archetypes we excel at.

capabilities.name: Data synchronisation
capabilities.desc: CRM ↔ ERP ↔ billing ↔ data warehouse. Two-way, with conflict resolution and retries.

capabilities.name: Document processing
capabilities.desc: Structured extraction of invoices, contracts and PDFs with AI, plus validation and auto-routing.

capabilities.name: AI orchestration
capabilities.desc: Multi-stage pipelines with several models, RAG and agents inside a single reusable flow.

capabilities.name: Smart notifications
capabilities.desc: Alerts to Slack, WhatsApp or email with AI prioritisation and contextual summaries.

capabilities.name: Custom integrations
capabilities.desc: Proprietary connectors when no official node exists — internal APIs, legacy systems, databases.

capabilities.name: Self-hosted n8n
capabilities.desc: Your own EU deployment with security, backups and enterprise-grade observability.

delivery.name: Process audit
delivery.desc: Map current flows, systems involved, volumes and pain points.

delivery.name: Architecture design
delivery.desc: We define flow structure, error handling, retries and monitoring.

delivery.name: Build & test
delivery.desc: We implement in an isolated environment with real test cases before touching production.

delivery.name: Launch & care
delivery.desc: Deployment, training and continuous support — including evolution when tools change.

faq.q: Why n8n and not Zapier?
faq.a: n8n is open-source, self-hostable, supports advanced JavaScript and has a fixed cost — it scales without surprises. For high volumes or sensitive flows, it's superior. For simple things, Zapier is still reasonable and we use it too.

faq.q: Can we self-host it?
faq.a: Yes. Deploying n8n on your own cloud or infrastructure is often our default for companies with data-sovereignty requirements.

faq.q: What if a tool changes its API?
faq.a: Live flows need maintenance. We offer a monthly maintenance contract covering API changes, migrations and optimisation.

faq.q: Does this replace my IT team?
faq.a: No. It complements them. IT maintains infrastructure; we build and maintain the specialised automation flows that require AI judgement or intensive integration.

faq.q: When will I see first results?
faq.a: A first production flow in 1–2 weeks. Larger projects ship in phases, always with visible value within 30 days.

consulting.name: Strategic consulting
consulting.sub: We help you decide where AI delivers real value and where it's a distraction. We audit your business, prioritise opportunities, validate use cases and hand over an honest, executable roadmap.
consulting.whatBody: The noise around AI makes many companies buy solutions before understanding the problem. We start the other way around: we understand the business, identify where the value is, and only then recommend what to build, buy or ignore.
forWho: You're evaluating AI solutions and need an independent technical view before signing contracts.
You have a leadership team that wants a clear 12–18 month AI strategy, not headlines.
You've invested in tools and don't see returns — you want to understand why and what to change.
You want to build an internal AI team and need judgement on profiles, tools and priorities.
consulting.capsLede: Actionable documents, not decorative slide decks. Every deliverable supports a decision.

capabilities.name: AI audit
capabilities.desc: Inventory of what you already have, data-quality analysis, risks and opportunities. A clean starting point.

capabilities.name: AI roadmap
capabilities.desc: 12–18 month plan with milestones, dependencies, costs and estimated ROI per initiative.

capabilities.name: Vendor evaluation
capabilities.desc: Technical analysis of market solutions with pros, cons and realistic alternatives.

capabilities.name: Prioritised use cases
capabilities.desc: List of cases with impact, effort, risks and concrete validation criteria.

capabilities.name: Governance & risk
capabilities.desc: Usage policies, data management, EU AI Act compliance and criteria for responsible adoption.

capabilities.name: Executive guidance
capabilities.desc: Regular sessions with your leadership team to steer the transformation with judgement.

delivery.name: Initial diagnosis
delivery.desc: Sessions with stakeholders, process analysis and current-stack review.

delivery.name: Analysis & priorities
delivery.desc: Internal work to model costs, risks and impact per case.

delivery.name: Executive deliverable
delivery.desc: Final document + leadership presentation with concrete recommendations.

delivery.name: Ongoing guidance
delivery.desc: Optional follow-up during execution: reviews, tweaks and support on key decisions.

faq.q: Are you vendor-agnostic or do you sell the same thing every time?
faq.a: We're vendor-agnostic. We have no commission deals with any provider. Our recommendation is often 'don't buy anything new — optimise what you already have'.

faq.q: What if the conclusion is that you don't need us?
faq.a: It's a valid result and we'll say so. We've closed consulting engagements recommending clients not invest in AI yet — because the timing wasn't right. Integrity is why people call us back.

faq.q: Do you only advise or do you also execute?
faq.a: Both. If you want, we execute the recommendations ourselves. If you have an internal team, we train and coach them. Often it's a mix.

faq.q: How much does it cost?
faq.a: Depends on scope. A small audit can be €4–6k. A quarterly executive engagement, €2–3k/month. Always agreed up front, no surprises.

faq.q: Do you sign NDAs?
faq.a: Of course. We work with sensitive information (financial, customer, strategic) and treat confidentiality as a non-negotiable baseline.

consulting.ctaTitle: Want an honest roadmap before investing?

training.name: Training & workshops
training.sub: Training programmes on applied AI for real teams. No buzzwords — practical exercises, your everyday tools and follow-up that outlasts the workshop.
training.whatBody: Most AI courses stop at 'what is an LLM'. We go to 'how to use an LLM inside your Excel, your CRM or your contract-review process'. Each session ends with a concrete tool or flow the participant can use the next day.
forWho: Manager and executive teams that need to make AI decisions and want sound judgement.
Operational teams (sales, support, finance, HR) that want to embed AI into their daily work.
Technical or data teams that want to move from 'playing with ChatGPT' to building productive solutions.
Companies that want to train the whole organisation with a common standard and coherent narrative.
training.capsLede: Every programme is tailored. These are the most common starting points.

capabilities.name: AI for executives
capabilities.desc: 1–2 executive sessions delivering strategic judgement. What to buy, what to build, what to ignore.

capabilities.name: Applied AI for teams
capabilities.desc: 4–8 week programme with practical tools for the specific role (sales, support, operations...).

capabilities.name: Technical bootcamp
capabilities.desc: Intensive training for engineering teams: prompting, agents, RAG, API integration.

capabilities.name: One-off workshops
capabilities.desc: Day-long workshops on specific topics: n8n, MCP, agents, AI ethics, advanced prompting.

capabilities.name: Continuous mentoring
capabilities.desc: Recurring 1:1 or small-group sessions to support real implementation on the job.

capabilities.name: Capability audit
capabilities.desc: Diagnosis of the team's current level and a measurable upskilling plan.

delivery.name: Needs assessment
delivery.desc: Interviews to understand the team, the roles and the real learning goals.

delivery.name: Programme design
delivery.desc: Tailored curriculum with agreed objectives, exercises and success criteria.

delivery.name: Execution
delivery.desc: On-site or remote sessions with materials, exercises and inter-session support.

delivery.name: Continuity
delivery.desc: Optional mentoring, reference materials and follow-up to consolidate the learning.

faq.q: Do you run open courses or only in-company?
faq.a: Mostly in-company and tailored — we believe it's the only way training translates into real change. Occasionally we run open sessions; ask about the next one.

faq.q: We work remote. Is that a problem?
faq.a: Not at all. We've delivered 100% remote programmes to European companies with results equivalent to in-person ones. We adapt dynamics and session length.

faq.q: What do participants need to know in advance?
faq.a: It depends on the programme. Courses for non-technical roles need nothing. Technical ones ask for basic Python or JS. We always run a 'session 0' to level the field.

faq.q: Do you deliver certificates?
faq.a: Yes, completion certificates and assessed certificates when there is evaluation. We're not an accredited body — the certificate's value is our technical reputation, not an official title.

faq.q: Subsidies (Fundae and similar)?
faq.a: For clients headquartered in Spain, yes. Training can be subsidised via Fundae and we support the paperwork.

training.ctaTitle: Want a team that can actually apply AI?

mcp.name: MCP servers
mcp.sub: We build Model Context Protocol servers so AI assistants — Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT — can securely access your databases, APIs and internal systems. The missing layer between AI and the enterprise.
mcp.whatTitle: The protocol that connects AI and the enterprise.
mcp.whatBody: MCP is the open standard (created by Anthropic) that lets an AI model read documents, query databases or execute actions inside your stack — with clear permissions and traceability. We build the MCP servers you need to use Claude or Cursor with your data, without it leaving your perimeter.
forWho: You want your team to query internal data (CRM, database, wiki) directly from Claude or Cursor.
You have internal APIs you'd like to expose to an AI assistant without rewriting them as 'tools' in every project.
You need a control layer between AI and enterprise systems, with auth, logs and granular revocation.
You're building a product that wants to offer integration with Claude Desktop, Cursor or other MCP clients.
mcp.capsLede: Custom MCP servers that connect AI to your company's operational reality.

capabilities.name: MCP for CRMs
capabilities.desc: Servers for Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive — so AI can read and update customer data with permissions.

capabilities.name: MCP for databases
capabilities.desc: Secure access to Postgres, MySQL, BigQuery or data warehouses, with restricted queries and auditing.

capabilities.name: MCP for internal APIs
capabilities.desc: Wrappers around your APIs so a model can invoke them as tools, without exposing credentials.

capabilities.name: Knowledge MCP
capabilities.desc: Servers that index wikis, drives, Notion or Confluence with per-user permissions respected.

capabilities.name: Action MCP
capabilities.desc: Servers that let AI execute actions: create tickets, send emails, update records, with confirmation.

capabilities.name: Distribution & security
capabilities.desc: Packaging, distribution (Smithery, npm, private registries), OAuth auth and usage policies.

delivery.name: Case analysis
delivery.desc: We understand what needs to be exposed, what permissions are required and which MCP clients will use it.

delivery.name: Server design
delivery.desc: We define tools, resources, prompts and auth policies.

delivery.name: Build & test
delivery.desc: Implement in TypeScript or Python, with regression tests and validation against Claude/Cursor.

delivery.name: Launch
delivery.desc: Deployment in your environment, client distribution and documentation for your team.

faq.q: What exactly is MCP?
faq.a: Model Context Protocol — an open standard from Anthropic so AI models can talk to external tools uniformly. It's like USB for AI: instead of bespoke integrations per model, you build one MCP server and Claude, Cursor and any compatible client can use it.

faq.q: Why not do it with traditional function calling?
faq.a: Function calling is per model and per project. MCP is reusable, has a standard protocol, supports auth, streaming and multiple clients at once. For companies it's much cleaner.

faq.q: Does it support auth seriously?
faq.a: Yes. MCP supports OAuth, API keys, per-user identities and granular policies. The servers we build always respect end-user permissions and don't operate as 'super-admin'.

faq.q: Where do we host the MCP server?
faq.a: Locally, in your cloud or on shared infrastructure depending on the case. For clients with sensitive data, we usually recommend self-hosting in the EU.

faq.q: When does MCP make sense and when not?
faq.a: It makes sense when you want to expose several systems to multiple AI clients while maintaining auth and traceability. For a single trivial integration between an agent and an API, classic function calling is simpler.

mcp.ctaTitle: Want to connect your AI to your stack?

web.name: AI-native websites
web.sub: We build websites and digital products where AI is part of the fabric — semantic search, assistants that understand the catalogue, recommenders, content generation. All with careful design and architecture that holds up.
web.whatTitle: Sites that think, not just decorate.
web.whatBody: Most corporate sites are static catalogues. We build sites where AI delivers concrete value to each visitor: assistants that know the product, semantic search that understands intent, personalised generation, recommenders. Always with strong performance and SEO.
forWho: You have a wide catalogue (products, articles, case studies) and need search that understands natural language.
You want visitors to have an assistant that answers about your offer, pricing or cases before they contact you.
You're building a SaaS where AI is part of the product and you need to integrate it into the public site and the product.
You want to refresh the website with modern design and, in passing, embed AI capabilities where they bring real value.
web.capsLede: Sites and products with AI integrated from the first prototype — not bolted on afterwards.

capabilities.name: Corporate sites
capabilities.desc: Fast, accessible, well-designed sites. Performance 95+, solid SEO, structured content.

capabilities.name: Semantic search
capabilities.desc: Search that understands intent, not just keywords. Over products, articles or documentation.

capabilities.name: Product assistants
capabilities.desc: Embedded chats/agents that know the catalogue and help users buy, compare or contact.

capabilities.name: Recommenders
capabilities.desc: Recommendation systems with embeddings or models trained on the client's data.

capabilities.name: Content generation
capabilities.desc: Pages or sections dynamically generated with AI — landing pages, descriptions, summaries.

capabilities.name: Apps & SaaS
capabilities.desc: Full web applications with embedded AI, auth, database and admin dashboard.

delivery.name: Discovery & architecture
delivery.desc: We define users, key flows, integrations and where AI brings real value.

delivery.name: Design
delivery.desc: Our own or adapted design system, hi-fi prototypes and iterative reviews.

delivery.name: Implementation
delivery.desc: Build on a modern stack, AI integrated from the start, performance and SEO standard.

delivery.name: Launch & evolution
delivery.desc: Deployment, measurement, optimisation and continuous evolution based on real data.

faq.q: What about typical WordPress sites?
faq.a: For very simple sites, WordPress is still valid. When we want performance, embedded AI and a good developer experience, we move to Next.js, Astro or equivalents. The decision depends on the case.

faq.q: Do you do design or just engineering?
faq.a: Both. If you have a designer, we work with their Figma. If not, we design — we're obsessive about detail and have our own system that we adapt to your brand.

faq.q: How does AI affect SEO?
faq.a: Well, if done right. We render critical content statically or via SSR so Google indexes it. AI adds personalisation on top, it doesn't replace the canonical content.

faq.q: Do you maintain the site afterwards?
faq.a: Yes, on a monthly retainer. It covers updates, security, feature evolution and monitoring. Without a maintenance contract, we offer ad-hoc support.

faq.q: What about user data?
faq.a: Everything we capture is declared in your cookie banner and privacy policy. We care about GDPR and dislike aggressive fingerprinting. If there is AI personalisation, it's transparent and revocable.

web.ctaTitle: Want a website that thinks with you?
